task: Produce a STRIDE threat model for a mobile banking app with biometric auth, P2P payments, and third-party integrations, including threat matrix, risk ratings, and prioritized mitigations

criteria.description: Evaluates comprehensive threat identification across all six STRIDE categories with mobile banking-specific attack vectors, requiring minimum threshold counts per category and specific technical depth that reflects documented attack patterns and regulatory compliance requirements
criteria.pass_condition: Minimum 4 distinct threats per STRIDE category (24 total), with at least 15 threats (60%) demonstrating mobile banking domain expertise (biometric bypass, cryptographic key extraction, API abuse, etc.) and at least 6 threats (25%) incorporating documented attack patterns from the past 12 months

sub_attributes.description: Systematic coverage across all six STRIDE categories with minimum viable threat counts
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 4+ threats per category with balanced distribution, 0.7 if 3+ threats per category with minor gaps, 0.4 if 2+ threats per category with significant gaps, 0.0 if any STRIDE category missing or <2 threats

sub_attributes.description: Domain-specific threat modeling demonstrating financial services and mobile platform expertise
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 60%+ threats show mobile banking expertise (jailbreak detection bypass, secure element attacks, PCI tokenization vulnerabilities), 0.7 if 40-59%, 0.4 if 20-39%, 0.0 if <20%

sub_attributes.description: Incorporation of current APT tactics and real-world attack vectors targeting financial mobile applications
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 25%+ threats reference current techniques (SIM swapping, SS7 attacks, supply chain compromises), 0.7 if 15-24%, 0.4 if 5-14%, 0.0 if <5%

pass_examples: Spoofing category includes: biometric template injection, FIDO authenticator cloning, certificate pinning bypass, and fake banking app distribution with detailed attack vectors
fail_examples: Generic spoofing threats like 'user impersonation' without mobile banking context or technical depth
criteria.research_basis: NIST SP 800-154 guidance on mobile threat modeling requiring platform-specific threat identification and FFIEC authentication guidelines mandating comprehensive attack vector analysis

criteria.description: Evaluates comprehensive alignment between STRIDE threat categories and financial services regulatory requirements (PCI DSS, PSD2, FFIEC, FIDO Alliance), including explicit compliance gap analysis and control mapping
criteria.pass_condition: Explicit mapping of at least 18 threats (75% of minimum 24) to specific regulatory controls with numbered citations, plus one compliance impact assessment per STRIDE category (6 total), identify at least 5 compliance gaps with remediation recommendations, and assess regulatory impact categories for non-compliance
pass_examples: Tampering threats explicitly reference PCI DSS 3.4.1 cryptographic key protection and include analysis of mobile secure element vulnerabilities against FIDO Alliance security certification requirements
fail_examples: Mentions 'regulatory compliance' generally without mapping specific threats to numbered control requirements or impact analysis
criteria.research_basis: PCI Security Standards Council Mobile Payment Acceptance Security Guidelines and EBA RTS on Strong Customer Authentication requiring explicit threat-to-control mapping for compliance validation

criteria.description: Evaluates the cryptographic protection mechanisms for biometric templates throughout their lifecycle, including secure enclave/TEE storage, template transformation techniques, and key derivation alignment with FIDO Alliance standards
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 4 specific cryptographic protection mechanisms (e.g., template hashing with salting, secure enclave storage, biometric cryptosystems, fuzzy extractors), specify minimum AES-256 encryption for template storage, and reference at least 2 FIDO Alliance UAF/WebAuthn specifications

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of biometric template encryption in secure hardware elements
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specifies AES-256+ with hardware key derivation and TEE/secure enclave isolation, 0.7 if mentions strong encryption but lacks hardware specificity, 0.4 if generic encryption discussion, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Evaluation of cancelable biometrics and template transformation techniques
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details fuzzy extractors, helper data separation, or template hashing with biometric-specific transforms, 0.7 if mentions cancelable biometrics concept, 0.4 if generic one-way functions, 0.0 if raw template storage

sub_attributes.description: Compliance assessment against FIDO Alliance UAF/WebAuthn biometric authenticator specifications
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if cites specific FIDO UAF authenticator metadata or WebAuthn authenticatorGetAssertion flows, 0.7 if mentions FIDO standards generally, 0.4 if references biometric standards without FIDO specificity, 0.0 if no standards reference

sub_attributes.description: Cryptographic key derivation and rotation for biometric template protection
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specifies PBKDF2/Argon2 key derivation with biometric entropy and rotation schedules, 0.7 if mentions key derivation from biometrics, 0.4 if basic key management, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Specifies AES-256-GCM encryption in iOS Secure Enclave with biometric template hashing using SHA-256 with device-specific salt, cancelable biometric transforms, and FIDO UAF authenticator metadata compliance
fail_examples: Generic mention of 'encrypted biometric storage' without cryptographic algorithm specification or hardware security element details
criteria.research_basis: FIDO Alliance UAF Authenticator-Specific Module API and ISO/IEC 24745 biometric information protection standard requiring specific cryptographic controls for template protection

criteria.description: Analyzes sophisticated biometric spoofing attack vectors and liveness detection bypass techniques specific to mobile device cameras and sensors, including presentation attacks and synthetic biometric generation
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 5 distinct presentation attack methods (photo replay, 3D masks, deepfake videos, infrared spoofing, synthetic fingerprints), assess effectiveness of mobile hardware countermeasures (depth sensors, pulse detection), and reference ISO/IEC 30107 PAD standards
pass_examples: Details photo replay attacks, 3D printed masks, deepfake video injection, pulse detection bypass, and synthetic fingerprint generation with assessment of iPhone Face ID depth sensor countermeasures and ISO/IEC 30107-3 PAD evaluation
fail_examples: Generic mention of 'biometric spoofing' without specific attack vectors or mobile hardware countermeasure analysis
criteria.research_basis: ISO/IEC 30107 Presentation Attack Detection standards and NIST SP 800-76 biometric data specification requiring comprehensive presentation attack analysis for financial authentication systems

criteria.description: Thorough analysis of mobile-specific attack vectors covering OWASP Mobile Top 10 threats with platform-specific technical details for iOS/Android security architectures
criteria.pass_condition: Must address at least 8 of 10 OWASP Mobile Top 10 threats with platform-specific technical details, include root/jailbreak detection bypass techniques, and analyze mobile app runtime protection mechanisms

sub_attributes.description: Coverage of OWASP Mobile Top 10 threat categories
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 8+ categories addressed with technical detail, 0.7 if 6-7 categories, 0.4 if 4-5 categories, 0.0 if <4 categories

sub_attributes.description: iOS/Android specific technical implementation details
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes iOS Keychain/Android Keystore analysis, platform-specific malware, and OS security feature bypass, 0.7 if partial platform specificity, 0.4 if mentions platforms without detail, 0.0 if generic mobile analysis

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of mobile app runtime application self-protection (RASP) and anti-tampering mechanisms
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details code obfuscation, runtime integrity checks, anti-debugging, and RASP bypass techniques, 0.7 if mentions some protection mechanisms, 0.4 if basic anti-tampering discussion, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Addresses M1 (Improper Platform Usage), M2 (Insecure Data Storage), M6 (Insecure Authorization) with iOS Keychain encryption analysis, Android root detection bypass via Magisk Hide, and runtime application self-protection evasion techniques
fail_examples: Generic mobile security threats without OWASP Mobile Top 10 mapping or platform-specific technical implementation details
criteria.research_basis: OWASP Mobile Security Testing Guide and NIST SP 800-124 Guidelines for Managing the Security of Mobile Devices requiring platform-specific threat analysis for enterprise mobile applications

criteria.description: Evaluates mobile cryptographic key storage, derivation, and lifecycle management using platform secure elements with analysis of key extraction attack vectors
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze iOS Secure Enclave and Android Hardware Security Module key protection, identify at least 3 key extraction attack vectors (memory dumps, side-channel, hardware), and specify key rotation requirements for mobile banking applications
pass_examples: Details iOS Secure Enclave key derivation with UID entropy, Android Hardware-backed Keystore attestation, memory dump attacks via cold boot, electromagnetic side-channel analysis, and 90-day key rotation for payment processing
fail_examples: Generic mention of 'secure key storage' without platform-specific hardware security module analysis or key extraction threat vectors
criteria.research_basis: NIST SP 800-57 Key Management Guidelines and FIDO Alliance security reference architecture requiring hardware-based key protection for high-value authentication systems

criteria.description: Comprehensive threat modeling of OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect implementations for mobile banking with analysis of token security, PKCE implementation, and authorization flow vulnerabilities
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE, identify at least 4 token-related threats (token theft, replay, injection, privilege escalation), and assess OpenID Connect security considerations for financial services

sub_attributes.description: Technical analysis of OAuth 2.0 authorization flows and PKCE implementation
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details authorization code flow with PKCE, token endpoint security, and mobile app registration, 0.7 if covers OAuth basics with some mobile specificity, 0.4 if generic OAuth discussion, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Comprehensive identification of token-related security threats
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies 4+ token threats (theft, replay, injection, privilege escalation) with attack scenarios, 0.7 if 3 threats, 0.4 if 2 threats, 0.0 if <2 threats

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of OpenID Connect security for financial services identity verification
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if addresses financial-specific OIDC considerations (identity verification levels, customer authentication), 0.7 if mentions OIDC with some financial context, 0.4 if basic OIDC discussion, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Analyzes OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE code challenge, identifies authorization code interception, access token theft via XSS, refresh token replay attacks, and scope privilege escalation with OpenID Connect financial identity verification requirements
fail_examples: Generic API authentication discussion without OAuth 2.0 flow specificity or token-related threat analysis
criteria.research_basis: RFC 6749 OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework, RFC 7636 PKCE, and OpenID Connect Core specification with financial services identity verification requirements per PSD2 RTS

criteria.description: Evaluates API gateway security controls including rate limiting, DDoS protection, and input validation mechanisms specific to high-volume mobile banking transaction processing
criteria.pass_condition: Must specify quantitative rate limiting thresholds for different API endpoints (authentication, payments, account queries), analyze at least 3 DDoS attack vectors (volumetric, protocol, application layer), and detail input validation controls for financial data
pass_examples: Specifies 5 authentication attempts per minute, 100 balance queries per hour, 10 P2P transfers per day rate limits, analyzes volumetric UDP flood and application-layer account enumeration attacks, with input validation for IBAN format and transaction amount limits
fail_examples: Generic mention of 'rate limiting and DDoS protection' without quantitative thresholds or banking-specific attack scenario analysis
criteria.research_basis: NIST SP 800-61 Computer Security Incident Handling Guide and OWASP API Security Top 10 requiring quantitative security controls for high-availability financial services

criteria.description: Deep analysis of payment card tokenization, P2P payment processing security, and transaction integrity mechanisms with EMV and PCI tokenization standard compliance
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze EMV payment tokenization, domain-restricted tokens, P2P payment flow security with at least 3 transaction integrity controls (digital signatures, timestamps, non-repudiation), and PCI tokenization standard compliance assessment

sub_attributes.description: EMV and PCI tokenization standard implementation analysis
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details EMV tokenization, domain-restricted tokens, and PCI DSS tokenization guidelines, 0.7 if covers payment tokenization with some standard reference, 0.4 if basic tokenization discussion, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Security analysis of peer-to-peer payment processing flows
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if analyzes P2P initiation, authorization, clearing, and settlement security with threat identification, 0.7 if covers P2P flow with some security analysis, 0.4 if basic P2P discussion, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Digital signature, timestamp, and non-repudiation mechanisms for payment transactions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details 3+ integrity controls (digital signatures, timestamps, audit trails, non-repudiation), 0.7 if 2 controls, 0.4 if 1 control, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Analyzes EMV payment token provisioning with domain restriction to mobile app, P2P payment flow from initiation through ACH settlement, digital signature validation, transaction timestamps, and audit trail generation for non-repudiation compliance
fail_examples: Generic payment processing discussion without tokenization standard specificity or transaction integrity control analysis
criteria.research_basis: EMVCo Payment Tokenisation Specification, PCI Tokenization Guidelines, and NACHA Operating Rules requiring specific security controls for electronic payment processing

criteria.description: Assessment of real-time fraud detection system integration with mobile banking application including behavioral analytics, device fingerprinting, and transaction monitoring
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 4 fraud detection techniques (behavioral analytics, device fingerprinting, velocity checks, geolocation analysis), analyze real-time decision engine integration, and assess false positive/negative impact on customer experience

sub_attributes.description: Comprehensive coverage of modern fraud detection methodologies
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies 4+ techniques (behavioral analytics, device fingerprinting, velocity checks, geolocation, machine learning), 0.7 if 3 techniques, 0.4 if 2 techniques, 0.0 if <2 techniques

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of real-time fraud decision engine integration with mobile app transaction flows
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details API integration, decision latency requirements, and escalation workflows, 0.7 if mentions real-time processing with some detail, 0.4 if basic integration discussion, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of fraud controls impact on legitimate customer transactions
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if analyzes false positive rates, customer friction, and adaptive authentication, 0.7 if mentions customer impact considerations, 0.4 if basic usability discussion, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Identifies behavioral analytics for typing patterns, device fingerprinting via hardware characteristics, transaction velocity checks, geolocation verification, with sub-100ms decision engine API integration and adaptive step-up authentication to minimize customer friction
fail_examples: Generic fraud detection mention without specific techniques or real-time integration analysis
criteria.research_basis: FFIEC Authentication in an Internet Banking Environment guidance and NIST SP 800-63B requiring multi-factor fraud detection for high-risk financial transactions

criteria.description: Implementation of quantitative risk scoring methodology using industry-standard frameworks with business impact weighting and exploitability analysis specific to mobile banking threat landscape
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement quantitative risk framework (DREAD, CVSS-adapted, or custom) with minimum 3 clearly defined scoring factors, numerical scores for at least 20 threats (80% of minimum 24), clear prioritization ranking with technical justification for top 10 threats, and qualitative business impact categories (High/Medium/Low)

sub_attributes.description: Sophistication and completeness of numerical risk scoring framework
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements DREAD, CVSS-adapted, or equivalent with 3+ factors and documented scale, 0.7 if 2 factors with clear methodology, 0.4 if basic numeric scoring, 0.0 if qualitative only

sub_attributes.description: Percentage of identified threats receiving quantitative risk scores
Integration of mobile banking business impact factors in threat prioritization
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes customer trust impact, regulatory penalties, revenue loss with monetary estimates, 0.7 if partial business impact analysis, 0.4 if mentions business context, 0.0 if technical-only

sub_attributes.description: Clear justification and ranking of highest priority threats with business context
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if provides top 10 ranked threats with business justification and remediation priority, 0.7 if top 5-9 threats ranked, 0.4 if some prioritization, 0.0 if no ranking

pass_examples: Implements modified DREAD scoring with Damage, Reproducibility, Exploitability, Affected Users, Discoverability factors, scores API authentication bypass as 8.2/10 due to high customer data exposure and $2M potential regulatory penalty, ranks in top 3 threats
fail_examples: Lists threats as 'high/medium/low' without quantitative methodology or provides numeric scores without documented framework or business impact consideration
criteria.research_basis: NIST SP 800-30 Risk Assessment methodology requiring quantitative analysis and FAIR (Factor Analysis of Information Risk) standard for monetary impact assessment in financial services

criteria.description: Integration of publicly available threat intelligence and documented attack patterns to inform risk prioritization with focus on tactics targeting financial institutions and mobile banking platforms
criteria.pass_condition: Must reference at least 3 threat intelligence sources (MITRE ATT&CK, public vendor reports, security advisories), map identified threats to documented attack techniques, and adjust risk scores based on attack frequency or sophistication factors
pass_examples: References FS-ISAC alerts on mobile banking malware, maps SIM swapping to MITRE ATT&CK T1111 Multi-Factor Authentication Interception, and increases risk scores for SMS-based authentication based on recent Lazarus Group campaigns targeting financial institutions
fail_examples: Generic mention of 'advanced persistent threats' without specific intelligence sources, techniques, or financial sector context
criteria.research_basis: MITRE ATT&CK for Enterprise framework and FS-ISAC threat intelligence sharing requirements for financial institution cybersecurity risk management

criteria.description: Analysis of sophisticated attack scenarios including SIM swapping, SS7/Diameter protocol attacks, supply chain compromises, and multi-stage attacks targeting mobile banking infrastructure
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze at least 4 advanced attack scenarios (SIM swapping, SS7/Diameter attacks, supply chain compromise, social engineering campaigns), detail multi-stage attack chains, and assess nation-state or organized crime threat actor capabilities

sub_attributes.description: Coverage of sophisticated attack scenarios beyond standard threat models
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if analyzes 4+ scenarios (SIM swapping, SS7 attacks, supply chain, social engineering) with technical detail, 0.7 if 3 scenarios, 0.4 if 2 scenarios, 0.0 if <2 scenarios

sub_attributes.description: Detailed analysis of multi-stage attack progression and kill chain methodology
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details complete attack chains with initial access, persistence, privilege escalation, and exfiltration stages, 0.7 if partial chain analysis, 0.4 if mentions multi-stage attacks, 0.0 if single-stage only

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of threat actor sophistication and resource requirements
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if assesses nation-state, organized crime, or insider threat capabilities with resource requirements, 0.7 if mentions threat actor types, 0.4 if basic actor discussion, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Analyzes SIM swapping attack chain: social engineering carrier support → SIM transfer → SMS OTP interception → account takeover → funds transfer, with assessment of organized crime group capabilities and $50K-$100K per campaign resource requirements
fail_examples: Generic advanced threat discussion without specific attack scenario detail or multi-stage progression analysis
criteria.research_basis: NIST Cybersecurity Framework and Lockheed Martin Cyber Kill Chain methodology requiring comprehensive analysis of sophisticated attack progression for high-value financial targets

criteria.description: Comprehensive security control recommendations mapped to identified threats with implementation guidance, cost-benefit analysis, and integration with existing security architecture using defense-in-depth principles
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide specific mitigation controls for 80% of identified threats, include implementation timeline and cost estimates for top 10 threats, map controls to NIST CSF or ISO 27002, and demonstrate defense-in-depth layering

sub_attributes.description: Percentage of identified threats with specific mitigation controls
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 80%+ threats have specific mitigation controls, 0.7 if 60-79%, 0.4 if 40-59%, 0.0 if <40%

sub_attributes.description: Practical implementation details including timeline and cost estimates
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if provides timeline and cost estimates for top 10 threats with implementation details, 0.7 if partial implementation guidance, 0.4 if basic recommendations, 0.0 if theoretical only

sub_attributes.description: Mapping of controls to established security frameworks
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if explicitly maps controls to NIST CSF, ISO 27002, or COBIT with control numbers, 0.7 if mentions frameworks with some mapping, 0.4 if generic framework reference, 0.0 if no framework alignment

sub_attributes.description: Demonstration of layered security control strategy
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if demonstrates multiple control layers (preventive, detective, corrective) with redundancy, 0.7 if shows some layering, 0.4 if mentions defense-in-depth, 0.0 if single-layer controls

pass_examples: Provides biometric spoofing mitigation: liveness detection (6 months, $200K), secure template storage (3 months, $50K), FIDO authentication (12 months, $500K) mapped to NIST CSF PR.AC-1 and ISO 27002 A.9.2.1 with defense-in-depth layering
fail_examples: Generic recommendations like 'implement strong authentication' without specific controls, implementation details, or framework mapping
criteria.research_basis: NIST Cybersecurity Framework Implementation Guidance and ISO/IEC 27002 security control implementation requiring specific, measurable security control recommendations with practical implementation guidance

criteria.description: Evaluates logical consistency between identified threats, assigned risk scores, and proposed mitigation strategies, ensuring highest-risk threats receive proportional mitigation investment and attention
criteria.pass_condition: Top 10 highest-risk threats must have corresponding detailed mitigation strategies with implementation priority alignment, and mitigation recommendations must demonstrate clear prioritization based on risk scores (high-risk threats get comprehensive controls, low-risk threats get basic controls)
pass_examples: API authentication bypass scored 8.5/10 receives $500K mitigation investment and 3-month implementation timeline, while low-risk UI spoofing (3.2/10) gets basic awareness training ($5K, 1 month)
fail_examples: Biometric spoofing identified as highest risk (9.1/10) but receives generic 'improve security' recommendation while lower-risk certificate pinning (4.3/10) gets detailed technical implementation plan
criteria.research_basis: Risk management coherence principles requiring proportional response allocation based on quantitative risk assessment outcomes

criteria.description: Assesses integration between regulatory compliance requirements and technical threat identification, ensuring compliance-driven threats receive appropriate technical analysis and vice versa
criteria.pass_condition: All referenced regulatory controls (PCI DSS, PSD2, FFIEC) must have corresponding technical threat analysis, and all high-risk technical threats must include compliance impact assessment with specific regulatory citations

sub_attributes.description: Technical threat analysis coverage for each referenced regulatory requirement
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if all regulatory controls have corresponding technical threat analysis, 0.7 if 75-99% mapped, 0.4 if 50-74% mapped, 0.0 if <50% mapped

sub_attributes.description: Compliance impact assessment for high-risk technical threats
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if all high-risk technical threats include specific compliance citations and impact analysis, 0.7 if 75-99% coverage, 0.4 if 50-74% coverage, 0.0 if <50% coverage

pass_examples: PCI DSS 3.4.1 cryptographic key protection requirement mapped to technical analysis of mobile secure element key extraction attacks, while API tampering threat includes specific PSD2 RTS Article 4 strong authentication compliance impact
fail_examples: Lists PCI DSS requirements without corresponding technical threat analysis, or identifies cryptographic vulnerabilities without assessing PCI DSS compliance implications
criteria.research_basis: Integrated risk management requiring alignment between regulatory compliance obligations and technical security threat assessment for comprehensive risk coverage

criteria.description: Evaluates integration between mobile platform security analysis and payment infrastructure security, ensuring mobile-specific vulnerabilities are properly assessed for payment transaction impact
criteria.pass_condition: Mobile platform vulnerabilities (root/jailbreak, app tampering, key extraction) must include analysis of payment transaction security impact, and payment infrastructure threats must consider mobile platform attack vectors

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of mobile platform vulnerabilities on payment transaction security
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if mobile vulnerabilities include specific payment impact analysis (transaction integrity, tokenization bypass), 0.7 if partial payment impact consideration, 0.4 if mentions payments generally, 0.0 if no payment impact analysis

sub_attributes.description: Integration of mobile attack vectors in payment infrastructure threat modeling
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if payment threats specifically consider mobile platform attack vectors (app tampering, device compromise), 0.7 if partial mobile consideration, 0.4 if basic mobile mentions, 0.0 if no mobile attack vector integration

pass_examples: Android root detection bypass analysis includes impact on payment tokenization security and transaction signing key protection, while P2P payment flow analysis considers mobile app tampering attack vectors
fail_examples: Mobile security analysis mentions jailbreak detection but doesn't assess impact on payment card token security, or payment analysis ignores mobile platform compromise scenarios
criteria.research_basis: Holistic mobile payment security requiring integrated analysis of platform security and payment infrastructure interdependencies for comprehensive risk assessment
